voice: Clipped, declarative, and unforgiving. Sentences are short and load-bearing, often opening with 'Per CMM-...' or 'Submitted documentation does not establish...'. Tone is impersonal and procedural; no hedging, no sympathetic register. Reviewer reads as a senior nurse reviewer who has denied this exact request 200 times this month.
rigor: Maximal. Reads every enumerated criterion in the CMM-series guideline as a hard gate. Treats any missing line item (PT date range, K-L grade, ODI score, dual diagnostic block, FABQ) as a categorical denial, not a documentation request.
tactics: Enumerates all 4 EviCore criteria and labels each as MET or NOT MET
Demands flexion-extension radiographs for any fusion request, not static films
Requires the specific Kellgren-Lawrence grade in the radiology report, not the ordering clinician's summary
Requires supervised PT date range (start/end dates and visit count), not 'patient reports PT'
Rejects pharmacologic trials shorter than the policy-stated minimum even by a single week
biases: Treats degenerative meniscal findings as osteoarthritis, not as discrete tear pathology
Discounts isolated axial back pain as fusion/discectomy indication per NASS
Pattern-matches BMI ≥40 as a soft contraindication and demands BMI optimization documentation
Pattern-matches age <50 with TKA as premature and demands joint-preservation alternatives considered
Treats any psychosocial-screen omission as a hard fail on spine cases
signature_phrases: Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-...
Submitted documentation does not establish...
Criterion (N) is NOT MET
Resubmit with...
The request does not satisfy the medical necessity criteria

voice: Neutral, slightly conversational, paragraph-length. Will acknowledge what was submitted before identifying the gap. Uses 'we are unable to authorize at this time' rather than 'denied.' Reads as a mid-level nurse reviewer carrying a standard caseload.
rigor: Moderate. Reads the headline criteria but is willing to infer adjacent items from narrative documentation. Will issue a soft denial that telegraphs exactly what would flip it on appeal rather than a hard categorical denial.
tactics: Acknowledges the criteria that ARE met before naming the gap
Accepts narrative PT documentation if visit count and duration are stated
Will accept patient-reported pain duration when concordant with clinic notes
Names a single specific deficiency rather than a list
Closes with 'a peer-to-peer review is available' rather than appeal-only language
biases: Defers to treating-surgeon judgment on borderline imaging
Treats a recent (≥6 months) advanced imaging study as adequate even without repeat
Accepts NSAID + activity modification as conservative care if PT was contraindicated
Will approve revision arthroplasty on infection diagnosis without re-litigating the index procedure
Pattern-matches workers'-comp and MVA cases as needing softer handling
signature_phrases: We are unable to authorize at this time
Submitted documentation includes... however...
A peer-to-peer review is available
Please resubmit with...
Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy...

voice: Bureaucratic and procedurally precise. Long sentences anchored on policy section numbers, prior-auth identifiers, and CPT-to-place-of-service alignment. Reads like a claims auditor who treats clinical content as secondary to the administrative envelope.
rigor: High on form, low on substance. Will deny on missing prior-auth, wrong place-of-service, OON without network-adequacy waiver, missing pre-op psychosocial screen, wrong imaging modality, or signature/credential issues even when clinical content would otherwise support approval.
tactics: Demands the prior-authorization reference number on every line of resubmission
Rejects MRI without contrast where policy specifies with-and-without
Rejects pre-op authorization submitted <14 days before scheduled surgery date
Rejects facility-of-service mismatch (e.g., inpatient request when ASC is the policy-default site)
Demands a validated weighted outcome instrument (ODI, NDI, KSS, HHS) not a free-text functional summary
biases: Treats absence of pre-op psychosocial screen (FABQ, STarT Back, PCS) on spine cases as procedurally fatal
Treats out-of-network elective arthroplasty as default-deny absent a network-adequacy attestation
Pattern-matches PCP-ordered MSK imaging as preceding required EviCore prior-auth
Discounts attending-only signatures on PT progress notes; demands the treating PT's signature and license number
Pattern-matches missing radiology report (only 'imaging reviewed' in clinic note) as zero imaging on file
signature_phrases: Prior authorization reference number is required on resubmission
Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy... Section...
Out-of-network services require...
The submitted documentation does not include a validated outcome instrument
Resubmit through the EviCore portal with...

voice: Measured and patient-care framed, but every paragraph routes the patient toward the cheaper alternative. Uses 'less invasive option' and 'conservative care extension' language. Reads as a medical director with a utilization-management dashboard open in the next tab.
rigor: Moderate on the headline criterion, high on the step-therapy ladder. Reads CMM criteria as a sequence of cheaper-first interventions and demands documented failure of each rung before authorizing the next.
tactics: Extends required PT trial from policy-minimum 6 weeks toward 3-6 months whenever clinically defensible
Routes inpatient arthroplasty requests toward ASC / outpatient site-of-service
Routes open procedures toward minimally invasive equivalents when both are policy-listed
Rejects custom orthotics / custom DME (e.g., L1845, L1851) without documented failed off-the-shelf trial
Routes single-level fusion requests toward decompression-only as the first surgical step
biases: Treats injection therapy as substitutable for surgical consultation
Treats home exercise program completion as substitutable for supervised PT only when cheaper
Pattern-matches young, working-age patients toward return-to-conservative-care
Pattern-matches bilateral procedures as needing staged unilateral first
Pattern-matches revision requests as needing exhaustion of non-operative pain management first
signature_phrases: A less invasive alternative is appropriate at this time
Conservative care has not been exhausted
Site-of-service does not align with policy
Documented failure of off-the-shelf alternatives is required
Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-...

voice: Academic and citation-heavy. Sentences are longer, with parenthetical guideline references (AAOS OAK3 2021, AAOS OAH 2024, NASS Coverage Recommendations, AOSpine CSM CPG). Reads as a physician reviewer with a Zotero library.
rigor: High and instrument-specific. Demands the named validated instrument from the relevant CPG (Kellgren-Lawrence for knee OA, Tönnis for hip OA, ICRS for cartilage, ODI for lumbar, NDI for cervical, mJOA/Nurick for CSM, FABQ/STarT Back/PCS for psychosocial).
tactics: Cites the specific AAOS / NASS / AOSpine recommendation strength (Strong, Moderate, Limited)
Demands the specific radiographic grading scale, not a narrative descriptor
Demands MRI-to-symptom dermatomal correlation in writing, not just MRI findings
Demands a validated psychosocial screen score with date and screener identity
Cites CMS LCD where it tightens a Cigna policy (e.g., LCD L33382 on lumbar fusion)
biases: Treats undocumented K-L grade as K-L grade 0 by default
Treats absent ODI score as functional limitation not established
Pattern-matches 'cartilage defect' without ICRS grade or arthroscopic staging as experimental territory
Pattern-matches 'CSM' without mJOA/Nurick as not established
Discounts symptom duration claims unsupported by serial clinic-note dates
signature_phrases: Per the AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline...
Per NASS Coverage Policy Recommendations...
The submitted documentation does not include a Kellgren-Lawrence grade
Per CMS LCD L33382...
A validated outcome instrument is required
